result.markdown: --- title: "[integrated_brier_score()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-function}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Integrated (time-averaged) Brier score across multiple time points. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/_metrics.py#L300-L393){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python integrated_brier_score(surv, survival_prob, times) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Summarizes Brier scores computed at multiple time horizons into a single summary metric via trapezoidal integration. This provides an overall calibration measure that doesn't emphasize any particular time point. **Use this to**: Reduce multiple Brier scores (one per time point) to a single number for model comparison. A single IBS score makes it easier to compare two models or report a single "calibration quality" metric. **Interpretation**: Same scale as Brier score (0 = perfect, 1 = worst). Values of 0.15-0.25 are typical for reasonable survival models; values > 0.30 suggest poor calibration. ::: ## Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [surv]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Surv]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A right-censored `Surv` response. [survival_prob]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Predicted survival probabilities, shape `(n_subjects, n_times)`. [times]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Evaluation times (must be at least 2 to define an interval). The IBS is computed as the area under the Brier-score curve from times[0] to times[-1], normalized by the time span. ::: ## Returns {.doc-returns} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Integrated Brier score (time-averaged). Lower is better. ::: ## Details {.doc-details} **Computation**: The integrated Brier score is $$ IBS = \frac{1}{t_{\max} - t_{\min}} \int BS(t) \, dt $$ Using trapezoidal rule to approximate the integral. This ensures the summary score balances contributions from all times without emphasizing early or late horizons. **Time scale sensitivity**: IBS weights contribution proportionally to time intervals. If you have many time points clustered early (e.g., 10 times in [0, 100] and 1 time at [1000]), early times dominate. Use evenly-spaced time points for balanced assessment. ## Examples {.doc-examples} Fit a Cox model and compute its integrated Brier score over a range of times: ::: {#59b4dd13 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) cox = gw.CoxPH().fit(y, lung[["age", "sex"]]) times = [180, 365, 540] surv_pred = cox.predict(lung[["age", "sex"]], type="survival", times=times, format="pandas") probs = surv_pred.iloc[:, 1:].to_numpy().T ibs = gw.integrated_brier_score(y, probs, times) ibs ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` 0.21262046811957802 ``` ::: ::: Compare two models via their integrated Brier scores. Lower is better: ::: {#94294079 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} # cox2 = CoxPH().fit(y, lung[["age", "sex", "ph.ecog"]]) # More covariates # surv_pred2 = cox2.predict(...) # ibs2 = gw.integrated_brier_score(y, probs2, times) # print(f"Model 1 IBS: {ibs:.3f}") # print(f"Model 2 IBS: {ibs2:.3f}") # print(f"Better model: {'Model 2' if ibs2 < ibs else 'Model 1'}") ``` ::: Compute integrated Brier score over a wide range of times to get an overall calibration assessment: ::: {#b6a8c079 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} times_wide = list(range(100, 700, 50)) surv_pred_wide = cox.predict( lung[["age", "sex"]], type="survival", times=times_wide, format="pandas" ) probs_wide = surv_pred_wide.iloc[:, 1:].to_numpy().T ibs_wide = gw.integrated_brier_score(y, probs_wide, times_wide) print(f"IBS over {len(times_wide)} time points: {ibs_wide:.3f}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` IBS over 12 time points: 0.200 ``` ::: :::
